pmm-1.title: Discover B2B ICP & competitive landscape
pmm-1.body: Foundation for everything in May. ICP is under discovery — validate mid-market vs enterprise based on early pipeline signals.

pmm-1.title: Run 3 B2B discovery interviews via Delivered
pmm-1.body: Use the existing Delivered interview process. Focus on decision-maker pain points and buying triggers for the B2B offer.

pmm-1.title: Define B2B ICP hypothesis — mid-market vs enterprise
pmm-1.body: Based on interviews + early pipeline data. Flag if segment needs to shift. Kathy to sense-check vs existing PMM knowledge.

pmm-1.title: Competitive landscape snapshot (Sofia leads Claude Code trial)
pmm-1.body: Sofia's tool evaluation: use Claude Code with scheduled tasks to pull and structure competitor intel. Pilot the workflow, don't just read the output.

pmm-2.title: Develop B2B positioning using AI-assisted workflow
pmm-2.body: First live test of the AI research → enablement process. AI produces first drafts; Sofia and Kathy review and refine.

pmm-2.title: B2B messaging framework v1 (AI first draft, PMM refines)
pmm-2.body: Feed April research into AI. PMM reviews for accuracy and tone. Source of truth: Confluence page.

pmm-2.title: B2B sales battle card(s)
pmm-2.body: AI-generated from messaging framework. Covers key competitors, objections, and differentiators. Sales team must be able to use it without a briefing.

pmm-2.title: Objection handler document
pmm-2.body: Common objections mapped to responses. AI drafts, Sofia validates against interview data.

pmm-2.title: Positioning review + sign-off with Shauna
pmm-2.body: Present messaging framework and enablement pack to Shauna. Get explicit sign-off before content production begins.

pmm-3.title: Positioning live + research cadence established
pmm-3.body: June is execution, not discovery. Positioning is locked and accessible. Research runs on autopilot.

pmm-3.title: B2B positioning published in Confluence
pmm-3.body: Final approved positioning live as the team source of truth. All campaign and content assets reference this page.

pmm-3.title: Competitor monitoring running on schedule (Claude Code)
pmm-3.body: Recurring scheduled task pulling competitor updates. Sofia reviews output weekly. Proves the AI research workflow in production.

content-1.title: Content production system: set up before producing anything
content-1.body: The system is the work — not the individual pieces. Map the brief-to-published workflow, agree what AI draft quality looks like, get it connected to Airtable and ClickUp before May.

content-1.title: Content workflow live in ClickUp
content-1.body: All requests, briefs, and approvals tracked in ClickUp with clear owners and due dates. Replaces Slack-based coordination. This is the habit change that makes everything else possible.

content-1.title: B2B content brief template in Airtable
content-1.body: Standard brief format that connects to Sofia's positioning work once it lands. Each B2B piece starts here — not in a Slack message. Enables the AI draft pipeline to pull consistent, structured inputs.

content-1.title: B2B content audit + gap list agreed with Sofia
content-1.body: Map existing assets to the B2B buyer journey. Identify what the launch needs that doesn't exist. Output: the May production queue, in priority order. Sofia reviews for PMM accuracy.

content-2.title: Produce B2B content through the AI workflow — testing the system is the goal
content-2.body: Every piece produced in May runs through the Airtable brief → AI draft → review → ClickUp approval workflow. The B2B content IS the test. Document what breaks.

content-2.title: AI draft pipeline: first full run
content-2.body: Brief from Airtable → Claude/AI tool drafts → Lana edits for accuracy → Tom approves. The first run reveals where the workflow breaks down. Document it — that's the point.

content-2.title: B2B blog (x2) + email sequence: three pieces run through the workflow
content-2.body: Not 'write two blog posts'. Run the brief through the pipeline, review the AI output, edit for brand voice, publish. Track actual editing time vs starting from scratch — that's the data point.

content-2.title: Delivered B2B launch feature: flagship content through the same workflow
content-2.body: Higher stakes than a blog. Tom writes the final version — but structure and draft come from AI. If this works, the workflow scales to Delivered's most important pieces.

content-3.title: B2B content approved + content system validated for scale
content-3.body: All June launch content is approved and ready. The system that produced it is documented and repeatable — not a one-time effort.

content-3.title: All B2B launch content reviewed and approved
content-3.body: Shauna final sign-off on all assets before go-live. Content is locked for launch week.

content-3.title: Webinar deck: AI-drafted from B2B positioning and early campaign data
content-3.body: First test of AI for presentation content. Tom drafts the webinar narrative from May's positioning work and early campaign signals. Not a template — a workflow test that feeds the 21 July proof point.

growth-1.title: B2B campaign ops setup
growth-1.body: Get the operational infrastructure in place so May build and June launch are not delayed by setup work.

growth-1.title: ClickUp campaign management live
growth-1.body: Campaign brief template active. All ABM programs tracked in ClickUp with owners and due dates.

growth-1.title: B2B ABM segment defined + accounts loaded in Airtable
growth-1.body: Define the B2B target account segment. Load accounts into Airtable. Set up B2B-specific tracking columns.

growth-1.title: B2B campaign brief template created
growth-1.body: One-brief format that can generate cluster variants in May. Includes ICP hypothesis, key messages, channel plan, and success metrics.

growth-2.title: Build B2B campaign assets
growth-2.body: Brief-to-campaign in May. One B2B brief → AI generates variants for each ABM cluster. Kaylee reviews; does not rewrite from scratch.

growth-2.title: B2B campaign brief signed off by Shauna
growth-2.body: Present the full campaign plan to Shauna — channels, budget, targeting, messaging, expected pipeline impact. Get explicit approval before building assets.

growth-2.title: Webflow B2B landing page (copy + design brief)
growth-2.body: AI drafts copy from positioning framework. Kaylee reviews for campaign fit. Colin builds. Must be live for June 2 campaign launch.

growth-2.title: LinkedIn campaign creative brief
growth-2.body: Kaylee writes brief, Colin designs ads. AI-generated cluster variants from one master message. 1:few format targeting B2B ICP.

growth-2.title: AI-generated message variants (one brief → cluster variants)
growth-2.body: The AI campaign workflow in action. One B2B brief → 3 cluster-specific variants. Kaylee reviews and approves each.

growth-3.title: B2B launch execution
growth-3.body: June is go-live. Everything built in May gets deployed. Sales team is activated. Pipeline tracking is on.

growth-3.title: LinkedIn campaign live
growth-3.body: Paid campaign targeting B2B ICP accounts. Cluster-specific messaging. Monitor daily for first 2 weeks.

growth-3.title: Email nurture campaign live
growth-3.body: 3-part sequence from May activated against B2B Airtable segment. Track open rates, clicks, and pipeline influence.

growth-3.title: Sales team briefed + enablement pack delivered
growth-3.body: Sofia and Kaylee jointly brief the sales team. Battle cards, one-pager, and objection handler delivered. Sales team must be ready before campaigns go live.

growth-3.title: Airtable B2B pipeline attribution tracking active
growth-3.body: Track campaign-influenced pipeline in Airtable. Define what "influenced" means upfront. Needed for webinar narrative in July.

growth-3.title: Pre-webinar campaign performance review
growth-3.body: Review first 3 weeks of campaign data. What is working, what needs adjusting. Input to webinar content — use real numbers.

tools-1.title: PMM → Sales enablement MVP: build and first test in April
tools-1.body: Don't wait until May. April's job is to get a rough deck out of the Confluence → Airtable → Gamma pipeline so May can be about refinement — not setup. Sofia owns the Confluence structure; Kaylee owns the Airtable and Gamma connection.

tools-1.title: Confluence B2B positioning page: structured for Gamma input
tools-1.body: The positioning page needs consistent, parseable sections — not free-form prose. Sofia creates the template: ICP, value prop, key messages, objections. This is what the Gamma pipeline reads from. Structure it before writing it.

tools-1.title: First pipeline test: Confluence → Airtable → Gamma → deck
tools-1.body: Run the full workflow once before May. The output will be rough — that's expected. What matters is identifying where the pipeline breaks. Document what Gamma got wrong and why. That list is the May brief.

tools-2.title: Refine the MVP and prove it — 5 approved decks by end of May
tools-2.body: April gave us a rough deck and a list of what broke. May fixes the input structure, runs the pipeline 5 times, and gets Sofia's sign-off on each output. If she's rewriting instead of reviewing, fix the Confluence input — not the deck.

tools-2.title: Revise Confluence template based on April test output
tools-2.body: What did Gamma get wrong? Fix the source — the Confluence page structure and data quality. Sofia owns the quality bar: if the output doesn't reflect real positioning, the input wasn't structured well enough.

tools-2.title: 5 B2B sales decks: pipeline produces, Sofia reviews
tools-2.body: Run the Confluence → Airtable → Gamma pipeline 5 times — one per B2B use case or segment. Sofia reviews each. Goal: Shauna can use one of these decks without asking Sofia to edit it first.

tools-2.title: Content + campaign workflows: first live runs in parallel
tools-2.body: Content: Airtable brief → Claude drafts B2B blog and email → Lana edits. Campaign: ClickUp brief → AI cluster variants → Kaylee approves. Both run in May alongside the PMM pipeline. The three workflows are independent but share the same Airtable backbone.

tools-3.title: All three workflows in production — no manual handoffs remaining
tools-3.body: Research → Content → Campaign. Each step passes through the system. If a handoff is still manual, it's a documented exception, not the default.

tools-3.title: Retro: what the system automated vs what stayed manual
tools-3.body: Honest numbers for the July webinar. Time saved, steps eliminated, output quality delta. The narrative Kaylee presents on 21 July is grounded in real data, not theory.

outcomes-1.title: Leadership aligned: B2B launch = proof of AI-powered GTM
outcomes-1.body: This is the framing agreed in the leadership session. B2B is not a separate project — it is how we demonstrate the AI system works.

outcomes-1.title: Shauna models ClickUp-first — not Slack
outcomes-1.body: How leadership works sets the standard for how the team works. ClickUp-first coordination from Shauna in April gives everyone the signal that the shift is real — and the permission to work that way too.

outcomes-1.title: OKRs documented as structured, queryable asset
outcomes-1.body: Campaign success metrics are defined from OKRs — not set informally each time. OKRs live in a structured format (Airtable or Confluence) that the campaign planning workflow can reference. Shauna to confirm format before May build.

outcomes-2.title: First AI workflow in production — B2B positioning + enablement
outcomes-2.body: By mid-May, Sofia has used AI to produce the first draft of real B2B enablement assets. The process is proven, not just planned.

outcomes-2.title: B2B positioning signed off — content production can proceed
outcomes-2.body: Shauna has approved the messaging framework. Tom has a locked brief. May 26 is the hard dependency for June launch content.

outcomes-3.title: B2B campaigns live — AI-powered marketing in production
outcomes-3.body: LinkedIn and email campaigns running against the B2B segment. Every asset was AI-assisted. This is the answer to "how is Marketing using AI?".

outcomes-3.title: Sales team enabled with AI-generated B2B assets
outcomes-3.body: Battle card, one-pager, objection handler — all AI-assisted, all in sales hands. Enterprise-led motion is supported by Marketing.

outcomes-3.title: Webinar delivery confirmed ready (21 July)
outcomes-3.body: Slides drafted, email campaign briefed, pipeline data ready to share. The July webinar is the public proof point for the AI GTM system.
